New Horizons Care Center

CCN: 535030 · Lovell, WY 82431 · Big Horn County

Overview

Address
1111 Lane 12, Lovell, WY 82431
Phone
3075485200
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
56 (66% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (hospital district)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since

Setting
Rural
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.86 3.04 4.90 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.92 0.53 1.16 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.43 0.68 0.54 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.52 1.84 3.19 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.34 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.40
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.84
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.31
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
29.8%
annual
RN turnover
14.3%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.08
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.78
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-09 5 4 1 24 1 24
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-10-19 6 2 4 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
31.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (13)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
10 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-04-02 2025-04-17
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-01-09 2025-01-30
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-09 2025-01-30
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-01-09 2025-01-30
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-01-09 2025-01-30
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-08-28 2024-09-09
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-08-28 2024-09-04
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-08-28 2024-09-04
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2023-10-19 2023-10-30
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. B 2023-10-19 2023-10-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2023-05-23 2023-06-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-08-18 2022-09-23
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2022-08-18 2022-09-23

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
